name: Bahrain
summary: Day 72 Sunday. **BAHRAIN MoI ANNOUNCED MAY 9 IT HAD ARRESTED 41 ALLEGED IRGC-LINKED INDIVIDUALS — FIRST MAJOR GULF-STATE INTERNAL ACTION AGAINST ALLEGED IRGC NETWORK DURING THE CYCLE.** Suspects had 'direct contact' with the IRGC and had collected funds intended to be sent to Iran. Investigations continue; further action anticipated (CONFIRMED — Khaleej Times primary, Washington Post, PBS NewsHour, Spectrum News, Anchorage Daily News, Inquirer/AP). Rights groups (HRW, others) argue Bahrain is using the war as cover to crack down on dissent generally — Bahrain hosts US Fifth Fleet and has historical Sunni-government / Shia-majority tensions where the regime has previously used 'Iran proxy' framing against Shia opposition (REPORTED — rights-group framing). **Two readings:** (a) substantive intelligence breakthrough — Bahrain has located IRGC funding/operational network and is dismantling it; (b) opportunistic crackdown using war as pretext — consistent with Bahrain's pattern. The truth may be both. Either way, this is a Gulf-state SIGNAL: Bahrain publicly aligning with the US blockade narrative against Iran, contrasting with Saudi Arabia's deniability-plus-Pakistan-alignment posture and Qatar's emergent IRGC-channel mediator role. **The Gulf-state response to Iran is now openly bifurcated:** Bahrain (pro-US, anti-IRGC), Saudi Arabia (deniable-pro-US-operationally, public-pro-mediation), UAE (continues hawkish per yesterday), Qatar (now openly mediating with IRGC contacts), Oman (silent mediator), Kuwait (denied airspace per WSJ scoop). PRIOR FRAMING (preserved): cumulative damage envelope from earlier cycle — 5th Fleet HQ, Bapco, ALBA, Batelco/AWS — still in effect.

active_triggers.condition: Second Gulf-state-led arrest sweep of alleged IRGC-affiliated network within 14 days (by 2026-05-24) following Bahrain's May 9 41-detentions — confirms US-led intel-sharing-and-disruption campaign against IRGC funding/operational architecture in Gulf

recent_signals.headline: BAHRAIN MOI ANNOUNCED MAY 9 IT HAD ARRESTED 41 ALLEGED IRGC-LINKED INDIVIDUALS — FIRST MAJOR GULF-STATE INTERNAL ACTION AGAINST ALLEGED IRGC NETWORK DURING THE CYCLE. Suspects had 'direct contact' with IRGC and had collected funds intended to be sent to Iran; investigations continue, further action anticipated. Rights groups argue Bahrain may be using war as cover to crack down on dissent generally (Bahrain hosts US Fifth Fleet, historical Sunni-government / Shia-majority tensions). Two readings: (a) substantive intelligence breakthrough; (b) opportunistic crackdown. Either way, Bahrain is publicly aligning with US blockade narrative against Iran. Gulf-state response to Iran now openly bifurcated: Bahrain (pro-US, anti-IRGC), Saudi (deniable-pro-US, public-pro-mediation), UAE (hawkish), Qatar (mediating with IRGC contacts), Oman (silent), Kuwait (denied airspace).
recent_signals.verification: CONFIRMED ACTION — 41 arrests, public Bahrain MoI statement (Khaleej Times primary, Washington Post + PBS + AP wire pickup multi-source); Iran has NOT commented publicly; rights-group alternative framing = REPORTED (HRW, others)

historical_summaries.summary: 1 signals. Iranian strikes targeted US Navy 5th Fleet HQ in Manama and Crowne Plaza Hotel

3 signals. Bahrain's Bapco declared force majeure after waves of Iranian strikes targeted energy installations | Air raid sirens activated across Bahrain early Sunday March 29 | IRGC struck ALBA aluminum plant (world's largest smelter) — 2 mildly injured, damage assessed

9 signals. IRGC missiles hit Batelco telecommunications facility in Hamala, Bahrain on April 1 — the facility hosts Amazon Web Services infrastructure | IRGC struck Batelco telecommunications facility in Hamala, Bahrain on April 1 — hosts AWS cloud infrastructure, servers rendered 'partly ino | CONFIRMED: Bahrain's Bapco Energies oil storage tank hit by Iranian drone attack on April 5
